leadText: This article is about the item. For a song called "Soda Pop", see Pokémon the First Movie (soundtrack). Soda Pop (Japanese: サイコソーダ Saiko Soda) is a type of medicine introduced in Generation I. It is a drink that restores a Pokémon's HP.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Manual activation 1.1.1 Generation I to VI 1.1.2 Generation VII onward 1.2 Other uses Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] Generation I to VI [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it heals the Pokémon by 60 HP . It is consumed after use. Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it heals the Pokémon by 50 HP . It is consumed after use. Other uses [ edit | edit source ] In the Kanto -based games, it can be given to a thirsty girl at the Celadon Department Store in exchange for TM48 ( Rock Slide ) R B Y , TM20 ( Safeguard ) FR LG , or TM09 ( Reflect ) P E . In the Generation I games, the player can give a Fresh Water , Soda Pop, or Lemonade to one of the Saffron City guards to be granted access to the city.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Restores HP that have been lost in battle by 60 HP. G S C Restores Pok é mon HP by 60. R S E Colo XD A fizzy soda drink that restores HP by 60 points. FR LG A fizzy soda drink. It restores the HP of one Pok é mon by 60 points. D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 A fizzy soda drink. It restores the HP of one Pokémon by 60 points. X Y OR AS A highly carbonated soda drink. When consumed, it restores 60 HP to an injured Pokémon. S M US UM A highly carbonated soda drink. When consumed, it restores up to 50 HP to an injured Pokémon. P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A highly carbonated soda drink. It can be used to restore 50 HP to a single Pokémon. S V ZA A highly carbonated soda drink. It can be used to restore 50 HP to a Pokémon.

Chimney Cable Car stations in Hoenn and on Routes 8 and 13 in Alola . It can be bought at a cheaper price of $ 250 from the vending machines at the Team Magma Hideout and Team Aqua Hideout . In the Generation V games, it can be sold to the gourmet maniac on Route 5 for $ 300. It is consumed after use. Generation VII onward [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag on a Pokémon, it heals the Pokémon by 50 HP . It is consumed after use. Other uses [ edit | edit source ] In the Kanto -based games, it can be given to a thirsty girl at the Celadon Department Store in exchange for TM48 ( Rock Slide ) R B Y , TM20 ( Safeguard ) FR LG , or TM09 ( Reflect ) P E . In the Generation I games, the player can give a Fresh Water , Soda Pop, or Lemonade to one of the Saffron City guards to be granted access to the city. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Soda Pop in A Pocketful of Adventures At normal purchase prices, the Soda Pop is the most cost-effective HP recovery item, recovering 1 HP per $ 6 spent. However, the Max Potion (as well as Full Restore and Revives in some cases) can potentially be more cost-effective if used under certain circumstances; the ideal situation for a Max Potion is a Blissey with maximum EVs and IVs in HP with 1 HP remaining, which would recover around 1 HP per $ 3.5 spent. Artwork from Generation I translates the name of the item as Psycho Soda. It also appears as Psycho Soda in Chapter 8 of Chuang Yi 's translation of Pokémon Pocket Monsters Soda Pop may be based on ramune , a type of drink originating from Japan. In the fifth episode of A Pocketful of Adventures , Yuki Koide appeared drinking a Soda Pop. [ 1 ] ↑ Cram Adventures Into a Pocket - Dogasu's Backpack
